Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jul-11-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing RE: Defendant UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC, RASIER-CA, LLC's Motion to Compel Arbitration.

Appearances:

Kyle R. Tracy, in person, TRACY LAW, PC, (626) 394-0843, for Plaintiff.

Laura Booth, via Zoom, SHOOK HARDY & BACON L.L.P., (949) 475-1500, for Defendants.

Ruling - ARGUED. The court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: the matter is taken under submission.***

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Court Reporter: Ryan Wheeler, T: (760) 805-3974, E: ryanwheeler91@yahoo.com. (302/RBU)

*** After the hearing, Order on Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration and stay is signed.***

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-15-2023 Continued To Aug-09-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jun-26-2023 Continued To Jul-11-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Kyle Tracy, Esq. 626-394-0843 Tracy Law for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces Counsel did not give timely notice of contesting the tentatative ruilng. Mr. Tracy confirms the late contest sent last Saturday. Mr. Tracy argues the delegation clause. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC, RASIER-CA,LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration.

Continued to July 11, 2023 at 9:30am. Mr. Tracy to notify Uber of continuance and to let Uber know if he is appearing to contest the tentative ruling on the next hearing date.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to GENERAL DENIAL
Off Calendar May-16-2023 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF MARA BRINA WEINER'S DEMURRER TO GENERAL DENIAL.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.

In response to the complaint, defendants filed a form general denial along with 26 affirmative defenses - a laundry list of legal labels. This is improper. Affirmative defenses must state supporting facts "as carefully and with as much detail as the facts" that support a cause of action in a complaint. (FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 384.)

Plaintiff also rightly notes that comparative negligence replaced comparative negligence in California 48 years ago and that statutes of limitations must be pled by section number.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to GENERAL DENIAL
Off Calendar Apr-12-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARA BRINA WEINER'S DEMURRER to GENERAL DENIAL.

The demurrer is ordered off calendar to enable compliance with CCP 430.41, if plaintiff still wishes to pursue it. Defendants state that plaintiff "did not identify" in a pre-filing meet-and-confer "which affirmative defenses were not pleaded with sufficient factual support." (Opp. 2:4-9.) This was contrary to CCP 430.41(a)(1).

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)
